name: DOM-less DI Host (display:contents)
summary: Confirms the layout-neutral provider-element pattern from the DOM-Less Composition research: a <di-scope> custom element that joins the injector tree to scope dependency injection but renders display:contents, so it adds no layout box — a flex/grid ancestor treats its children as its own. display:contents keeps the element in the DOM tree (the injector chain walks DOM ancestors, so a descendant still resolves the provider declared on the scope) while removing its box from layout: DOM-present, layout-absent. A conformance section asserts both halves live (computed display:contents, scoped provider resolves inside but not outside, children share the flex row equally) plus a control proving a plain <div> wrapper breaks the same row.
description:
Concept

Confirms the layout-neutral provider-element pattern from the DOM-Less Composition research: a <di-scope> element that joins the injector tree to scope dependency injection, but renders display: contents so it adds no layout box.

DOM-present, layout-absent

display: contents keeps the element in the DOM tree — the injector chain walks DOM ancestors, so a descendant still resolves the provider declared on the scope — while removing its box from layout, so a flex/grid ancestor lays out the scope's children as if they were its own.

Verified, with a control

The conformance section asserts the computed display:contents, that a descendant resolves the scoped provider while an element outside does not (isolation), and that the four flex items share the row equally across the scope boundary. A control proves the pattern is load-bearing: a plain <div> wrapper pulls its children out of the same flex row.
